hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model is trained to align SMILES and 3D electron density grids, then the learned 3D embeddings will be invariant to molecular rotations—enabling accurate retrieval of rotated conformers, higher multi-pose retrieval rates and tighter HOMO-based clustering and improved linear-probe QM9 property prediction compared to unimodal 3D and SE(3)-equivariant baselines—because the rotation-agnostic SMILES modality provides a supervisory signal that encourages the 3D encoder to ignore pose.
